Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization: A Training-Free Adaptive Framework
Short Hypothesis: Curvature-aware spectral analysis coupled with adaptive routing mechanisms can enhance the performance of mixed-precision quantization without requiring extensive training, leading to robust model deployment in resource-constrained environments.
Related Work: Current mixed-precision quantization frameworks, such as EMQ and InfoQ, focus on training-free proxies or local sensitivity heuristics. However, these approaches often overlook the global effects of quantization and the underlying geometric properties of model parameters. Our approach integrates curvature-aware analysis and adaptive routing, filling a crucial gap by formalizing the relationship between quantization, geometric properties, and model performance.
Abstract: Mixed-Precision Quantization (MPQ) is crucial for deploying deep neural networks in resource-constrained environments, yet existing methods often rely on heuristic or training-based approaches that limit efficiency and scalability. In this proposal, we introduce a novel training-free framework for MPQ that leverages curvature-aware spectral analysis to adaptively route quantization strategies based on the geometric properties of model parameters. Our framework operates by quantifying the curvature of the loss landscape to inform bit-width allocation across layers, optimizing the trade-off between model size and accuracy. We hypothesize that by understanding the geometric relationships within the model's parameter space, we can significantly enhance the performance of quantized models while maintaining robustness. We will validate this hypothesis through a series of experiments on standard benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach compared to state-of-the-art mixed-precision methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware routing on quantization performance using various architectures (e.g., ResNet, ViT) on ImageNet.
Compare the proposed framework against state-of-the-art methods (like EMQ and InfoQ) in terms of accuracy, computational efficiency, and memory usage.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the effects of curvature-aware analysis on quantization outcomes and model performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the assumption that curvature can effectively guide quantization decisions across all model types. Limitations may arise from the complexity of accurately computing curvature in high-dimensional spaces, which could introduce overhead. Additionally, while training-free methods offer efficiency, they may not achieve the same performance as fully trained quantization frameworks.
